title: Auth.js v5 signIn/signOut fails because server and client imports are swapped
summary: Importing signIn from next-auth/react in a server component (or from @/auth in a client component) causes runtime errors.
mistake: Importing `signIn` or `signOut` from `next-auth/react` in a Server Component or Server Action. Auth.js v5 has TWO separate signIn/signOut exports: server-side from `@/auth` (the project's auth config file) and client-side from `next-auth/react`. Using the client import on the server throws 'signIn is not a function' or a React hook error. Using the server import on the client throws a module resolution error or attempts to run server-only code in the browser.
instead: In Server Components and Server Actions, import from the project's auth config: `import { signIn, signOut } from '@/auth'`. In Client Components (files with 'use client'), import from the React package: `import { signIn, signOut } from 'next-auth/react'`. Never cross these boundaries. Additionally, do not wrap server-side signIn/signOut in try/catch — they intentionally throw a NEXT_REDIRECT which must propagate.
